title: Sapling params loader: SaplingParamPaths → (SpendParameters, OutputParameters)
description:
Implement load_sapling_params(paths: &SaplingParamPaths) -> Result<(SpendParameters, OutputParameters), TxBuildError>.

The SaplingParamPaths struct (already in wallet/src/params.rs) holds the disk paths to sapling-spend.params and sapling-output.params. The zcash_primitives or sapling crate provides a way to load these into SpendParameters and OutputParameters for use with the builder's SpendProver and OutputProver traits.

Steps:
1. Spawn a research subagent to find the exact loading function — look for sapling::circuit::SpendParameters::read() or similar in ~/.cargo/git/checkouts/sapling-crypto-*/src/
2. Read each param file into bytes (std::fs::read)
3. Construct SpendParameters and OutputParameters
4. Return the pair

Defensive rules:
- File not found → TxBuildError::ParamsNotLoaded('sapling-spend.params not found at PATH — run  to download')
- File corrupt (read fails) → TxBuildError::ParamsNotLoaded with path and error
- Parse error → TxBuildError::ParamsNotLoaded with details
- Never load both files if the first fails — fail fast
- Re-verify BLAKE2b hash after loading (use existing verify_blake2b from params.rs) → TxBuildError::ParamsNotLoaded('hash mismatch — file may be corrupt, delete and re-download')

Tests:
- Mock params: use a tempdir with valid param bytes; confirm Ok is returned
- Missing file: confirm TxBuildError::ParamsNotLoaded
- Corrupt file (truncated bytes): confirm TxBuildError::ParamsNotLoaded, no panic
- Hash mismatch: write valid bytes but fake a bad expected hash → TxBuildError::ParamsNotLoaded
Note: actual param files are large (~50MB each) — use a test double or read the research subagent output to determine if a minimal valid params struct can be created programmatically for tests.
acceptance_criteria: load_sapling_params succeeds with a valid params dir; all error paths return TxBuildError; hash re-verification is present
